skill.name: gws-drive-upload
skill.description: Upload local files to Google Drive with automatic MIME metadata using the gws CLI.
skill.summary: Uploads a local file to Google Drive with optional parent folder and target filename settings.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell execution, path traversal, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance patterns. Review found the weak cryptography, network reconnaissance, and path traversal alerts are false positives from Markdown text and relative documentation links, while the external command usage is an intended CLI upload workflow. The skill has medium risk because it can upload local files to Google Drive and must only run after explicit user confirmation.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Upload Command Can Move Local Files to Drive
medium_findings.description: The skill documents running gws drive +upload with a local file path. This is an intended Google Workspace operation, but it can transfer local data to a cloud account, so agents must confirm the exact file and destination before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples directly show a cloud upload of local files, and the file itself warns that this is a write command requiring user confirmation. The risk is legitimate but appears intentional rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Links Flagged as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The ../ paths point to adjacent skill documentation for shared Google Workspace guidance. No file read, write, or traversal logic is present in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only traversal-like strings are Markdown references to neighboring SKILL.md files. There is no executable code that consumes these paths.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain ordinary metadata or Markdown table text, not cryptographic code. No hash function, encryption routine, or security decision is implemented in this skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines do not contain a weak algorithm call or any cryptographic operation. The finding appears to match substrings inside documentation text.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Alert Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The flagged line explains that a filename is inferred from the local path unless a name is supplied. It does not perform scanning, probing, host discovery, or network enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain usage guidance about a filename option. No reconnaissance tool or network target appears in the reviewed content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented External Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs use of the gws CLI to upload a user-selected local file to Google Drive. This is expected for the skill, but it should be treated as a user-approved external command.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicitly documented and is central to the skill purpose. The security impact depends on the file path and Drive destination chosen by the user.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Path Input
dangerous_patterns.description: The required <file> argument identifies a local file to upload. Agents should avoid broad paths, hidden files, credentials, and files not explicitly requested by the user.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The required argument is clearly a local file path. There is no malicious handling shown, but incorrect use could disclose local data.

content.value_statement: Teams need a repeatable way to upload local files to Google Drive without guessing metadata. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through the gws CLI upload command with file, folder, and naming options.
actual_capabilities: Shows the gws drive +upload command for uploading one local file.
Supports choosing a parent Google Drive folder by folder ID.
Supports setting a target filename with the --name option.
Relies on automatic MIME type detection during upload.
Points users to shared Google Workspace authentication and global flag guidance.
limitations: Requires the gws CLI to be installed and authenticated before use.
Uploads one specified local file per command invocation.
Does not create folder IDs or choose Drive destinations automatically.
Requires user confirmation because it writes data to Google Drive.

use_cases.title: Upload Reports to a Team Folder
use_cases.description: Upload a finished local report to a known Google Drive folder with the original filename preserved.

use_cases.title: Publish Renamed Data Exports
use_cases.description: Upload a generated CSV or spreadsheet export and assign a clearer Drive filename during the upload.

use_cases.title: Prepare Shared Client Deliverables
use_cases.description: Move approved local deliverables into a client Drive folder after confirming the file and folder ID.

prompt_templates.title: Upload One File
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner upload with the default filename.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the gws-drive-upload skill to upload ./report.pdf to Google Drive. Confirm the file with me before running the command.

prompt_templates.title: Upload to Folder
prompt_templates.scenario: Upload a file to a specific Drive folder.
prompt_templates.prompt: Upload ./report.pdf to Google Drive folder FOLDER_ID using gws drive +upload. Confirm the destination before executing.

prompt_templates.title: Upload With New Name
prompt_templates.scenario: Upload a file and set a Drive filename.
prompt_templates.prompt: Upload ./data.csv to Google Drive as Sales Data.csv. Ask me to confirm the local file and target filename first.

prompt_templates.title: Validate an Upload Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before executing a write command.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this upload plan: local file ./deliverable.pdf, parent folder FOLDER_ID, target name Final Deliverable.pdf. Identify any missing confirmation before running it.

output_examples.input: Upload ./report.pdf to my Drive folder FOLDER_ID.
output_examples.output: I will confirm the local file ./report.pdf and the destination folder FOLDER_ID before running the Drive upload command.

output_examples.input: Upload ./data.csv and call it Sales Data.csv in Drive.
output_examples.output: The upload will use ./data.csv as the source file and Sales Data.csv as the Drive filename after you approve the write action.

output_examples.input: Can this upload every file in my downloads folder?
output_examples.output: This skill documents one file per upload command. Select the exact file to avoid uploading unintended local data.

best_practices: Confirm the exact local file path before running the upload command.
Verify the parent folder ID when uploading to a shared or client folder.
Use --name when the Drive filename should differ from the local filename.
anti_patterns: Do not upload files that may contain secrets or credentials without explicit approval.
Do not guess folder IDs or upload to an unclear destination.
Do not run the write command before the user confirms the file and destination.

faq.question: What does this skill upload?
faq.answer: It uploads one local file specified in the command to Google Drive.

faq.question: Can I choose a destination folder?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide a Google Drive parent folder ID with the --parent option.

faq.question: Can I rename the file during upload?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the --name option to set the target filename in Drive.

faq.question: Does it detect the MIME type?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill states that MIME type is detected automatically.

faq.question: Does it require authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. It depends on shared Google Workspace authentication guidance and the gws CLI.

faq.question: Is user confirmation required?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill marks the upload as a write command and says to confirm before executing.
